title: Winey, Silky, Kid-friendly Peruvian flour Rocoto Relleno
description: A Peruvian dish featuring a wine-infused, silky filling inside a hollowed-out flour tortilla. Kids and adults alike will enjoy this unique twist on a classic recipe.

ingredients.name: milk
ingredients.notes: 2% or low-fat milk is recommended for a kid-friendly option.

ingredients.name: grated cheddar cheese
ingredients.notes: low-sodium cheddar cheese is recommended for kids.

ingredients.name: grated carrot
ingredients.notes: shredded carrots add a sweet and crunchy texture.

ingredients.name: black beans
ingredients.notes: canned black beans are a convenient option.

ingredients.name: onion powder
ingredients.notes: use a kid-friendly seasoning like onion powder.

ingredients.name: red wine vinegar
ingredients.notes: a small amount of wine adds a unique flavor.

ingredients.name: peruvian aji amarillo pepper
ingredients.notes: use a kid-friendly hot sauce like sriracha for an alternative.

ingredients.name: cinnamon
ingredients.notes: a pinch of cinnamon balances the flavors.

ingredients.name: ginger
ingredients.notes: a pinch of fresh ginger adds a unique flavor.

ingredients.name: eggs
ingredients.notes: beaten eggs help bind the filling.

ingredients.name: ricotta cheese
ingredients.notes: low-fat ricotta cheese is a kid-friendly option.

instructions: Cut the flour tortillas in half lengthwise and hollow out the centers, leaving 1/4 inch of the tortilla intact.

In a large bowl, mix together the grated carrot, ground beef, canned diced tomatoes, onion powder, salt, red wine vinegar, olive oil, peruvian aji amarillo pepper, cinnamon, ginger, eggs, ricotta cheese, paprika, and dried oregano.
Stuff each tortilla shell with the filling, dividing it evenly among the tortillas.
Place the stuffed tortillas on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and bake for 15 minutes or until the cheese is melted and the filling is heated through.
